channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Witkoff, Kushner to head to new Iran peace talks in Pakistan, White House says
item.description: Witkoff and Kushner will attend new Iran peace talks in Pakistan. Live Updates: Witkoff, Kushner to head to new Iran peace talks in Pakistan, White House says CBS News.

item.title: US appeals court rejects Trump’s ban on asylum seekers, teeing up appeal
item.description: A US appeals court rejected Trump's asylum seeker ban. Judges say Trump's order for swift removal at the border 'cast aside federal laws affording' right to seek asylum. US appeals court rejects Trump’s ban on asylum seekers, teeing up appeal.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire amid Hormuz uncertainty
item.description: Israel and Lebanon extended their ceasefire amid uncertainty in the Hormuz region. Live updates: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire amid Hormuz uncertainty NBC News.

item.title: US Justice Department Allows Use Of Firing Squads For Federal Executions
item.description: The US Justice Department approved firing squads for federal executions. The death penalty is normally carried out at the state level in the United States. US Justice Department Allows Use Of Firing Squads For Federal Executions.

item.title: EU trade chief seeks 'positive traction' on US steel tariffs
item.description: The EU trade chief seeks to resolve US steel tariffs. EU trade chief seeks 'positive traction' on US steel tariffs Iosco County News Herald.

item.title: Justice Department drops investigation into Fed Chair Powell
item.description: The Justice Department dropped its investigation into Fed Chair Powell. The closing of the investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell removes an obstacle to the appointment of Kevin Warsh, US President Donald Trump's pick for head of the central bank.

item.title: Trump government extends Jones Act waiver by 90 days to dampen oil prices
item.description: The US extended the Jones Act waiver by 90 days to curb fuel price spikes before the midterm elections. Move is part of a broader US push to curb politically sensitive fuel price spikes before November’s midterm elections.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Trump sending envoys to Pakistan for talks with
item.description: Trump is sending envoys to Pakistan for talks with Iran's foreign minister. US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Trump sending envoys to Pakistan for talks with Iran FM; Vance put on standby The Times of India.

item.title: Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international law, UN warns
item.description: The UN warned that Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international law. UN report says Hezbollah rocket attacks on Israel may have breached international humanitarian law. Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international law, UN warns.

item.title: Israeli ambitions confront US dictates in Iran and Lebanon
item.description: The US imposed ceasefires with Iran and Lebanon on Israel. Ceasefires with Iran and Lebanon appear imposed by the US rather than negotiated by Israel, much to latter's unease. Israeli ambitions confront US dictates in Iran and Lebanon.

item.title: Trump Extended the Iran War Ceasefire. Now What?
item.description: Trump extended the Iran war ceasefire, a move seen as imposed by the US. Now What? Council on Foreign Relations.

item.title: US, Iran Prepare For 2nd Round Of Talks In Pak Without Vance, Ghalibaf
item.description: The US has confirmed that two key officials will fly to Pakistan tomorrow for talks with Iran. The talks aim to address the ongoing conflict. US Confirms Talks With Iran, 2 Key Officials To Fly To Pak Tomorrow.

item.title: Trump administration to prioritise seeking death penalty, use firing squads
item.description: The US Justice Department plans to prioritize seeking the death penalty in federal cases, with the option to use firing squads in some executions. Critics warn of innocent people being put on death row due to capital punishment's controversy.

item.title: Israel poses direct threat to global security: Turkish FM Fidan
item.description: Turkish Foreign Minister Mevlut Cavusoglu said Israel poses a direct threat to global security. He made the statement during a visit to a NATO meeting in Brussels, without elaborating on the reasons behind his assertion.

item.title: No 10 says Falklands sovereignty rests with UK after report of US 'review'
item.description: The UK government insists that Falklands sovereignty rests with the UK, following a report of a US review of its position on the islands. The report reportedly suggested a change in position in retaliation for the UK's decision not to join the Iran war.

item.title: Trump Sending 2 Key Officials To Pak For Talks With Iran: Report
item.description: Trump is sending officials to Pakistan for talks with Iran. The Iranians have not confirmed if they are going to meet with the US delegation. Trump Sending 2 Key Officials To Pak For Talks With Iran: Report.

item.title: Vance on 'standby' as Witkoff, Kushner to reach Pakistan on April 25 for talks with Iran, White
item.description: Iran's foreign minister is leading a delegation to Islamabad for talks with Pakistani officials. The Iranian delegation will discuss regional developments and the war situation. Iranian delegation led by FM Araghchi to reach Islamabad tonight; to “hold bilateral consultations on developments in the region, as well as the latest situation” in the war.

item.title: Zelenskyy visits Saudi Arabia, Putin weighs G20 attendance
item.description: Zelenskyy visited Saudi Arabia as Putin considers attending the G20. Russian President Putin "may or not may not" attend the G20 in Miami in December, says the Kremlin. Meanwhile, after securing an EU loan, Ukrainian President Zelenskyy continued his diplomatic offensive in Saudi Arabia.

item.title: As Putin Orders That the Economy Be Fixed, Russia Grasps for Solutions
item.description: Russia's central bank cut interest rates again amid wartime spending strains. Another interest-rate cut highlights the narrowing path for the country’s central bank amid the strains of immense wartime spending.

item.title: Pirates hijack oil tanker off the coast of Somalia
item.description: Pirates hijacked an oil tanker off the coast of Somalia. This incident marks a resurgence of piracy in the Indian Ocean, an area once notorious for such attacks. Piracy, once notorious in this stretch of the Indian Ocean, has made a comeback in recent years.
